sanskrit: बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते । वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ॥
hindiTranslation: बहुत जन्मोंके अन्तमें अर्थात् मनुष्यजन्ममें सब कुछ परमात्मा ही है, ऐसा जो ज्ञानवान् मेरे शरण होता है, वह महात्मा अत्यन्त दुर्लभ है ॥
englishTranslation: After many births, the wise one surrenders to Me, knowing that Vasudeva is everything. Such a great soul is very rare.
essence: Deep seeing ends in surrender to the one reality in all things.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Real understanding ripens over many lives. At its peak, a person sees that everything is the divine and naturally turns in surrender.
simpleInsight: There is relief in not forcing faith; deep seeing itself becomes surrender.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna explains why some people truly know Him. After many births, rare wisdom matures into surrender: the person sees Vasudeva in all things and comes home to Krishna.
modernRelevance: You keep searching for one more fix, one more answer, one more sign. Then a quiet certainty lands: nothing is separate, and the need to grasp begins to loosen.
translationLiteral: बहूनाम् (of many) / जन्मनाम् (births) / अन्ते (at the end) / ज्ञानवान् (the wise one) / माम् (to Me) / प्रपद्यते (surrenders) / वासुदेवः (Vasudeva) / सर्वम् (everything) / इति (thus) / सः (that) / महात्मा (great soul) / सुदुर्लभः (very rare)
insight: It shifts the mind from fragmented seeking to whole-hearted surrender.

quoteVariants.text: After many lives, wisdom bows to the divine. Everything is Vasudeva.
Understanding ends in surrender: everything is the divine.
